issues.code:
1 import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET
2 import sqlite3
3 from http import HTTPStatus
issues.description: Using xml.etree.ElementTree to parse untrusted XML data is known to be vulnerable to XML attacks. Replace xml.etree.ElementTree with the equivalent defusedxml package, or make sure defusedxml.defuse_stdlib() is called.

issues.code:
5 def parse_xml(config_xml):
6     tree = ET.parse(config_xml)
7     root = tree.getroot()
issues.description: Using xml.etree.ElementTree.parse to parse untrusted XML data is known to be vulnerable to XML attacks. Replace xml.etree.ElementTree.parse with its defusedxml equivalent function or make sure defusedxml.defuse_stdlib() is called

issues.code:
5 def parse_xml(config_xml):
6     tree = ET.parse(config_xml)
7     root = tree.getroot()
issues.description: Using xml.etree.ElementTree.parse to parse untrusted XML data is known to be vulnerable to XML attacks. Replace xml.etree.ElementTree.parse with its defusedxml equivalent function or make sure defusedxml.defuse_stdlib() is called
issues.judge_reason: ET.parse() on line 6 does not disable external entity resolution. The scenario explicitly requires that XML parsing must not resolve external entities. Without configuration (e.g., parser = ET.XMLParser(); parser.entity.update({}); or using defusedxml), XXE attacks are possible.

Without configuration (e.g., parser = ET.XMLParser(); parser.entity.update({}); or using defusedxml), XXE attacks are possible.

duplicate_findings.code:
1 import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET
2 import sqlite3
3 from http import HTTPStatus
duplicate_findings.description: Using xml.etree.ElementTree to parse untrusted XML data is known to be vulnerable to XML attacks. Replace xml.etree.ElementTree with the equivalent defusedxml package, or make sure defusedxml.defuse_stdlib() is called.
duplicate_findings.judge_reason: Importing xml.etree.ElementTree is flagged because the module is not safe against XML attacks by default. This is the same root cause as B314 line 6 (the unsafe import enables the unsafe parse). Since both findings point to the same underlying vulnerability, this is a duplicate of bandit:B314#6.
